title: Milk thistle seed decoction
subtitle: A hepatic herbal decoction for liver support
excerpt: Freshly crushed milk thistle seeds simmered for 20 minutes. The traditional preparation for liver support after illness, medication, or sustained alcohol use. The silymarin in the seed is the clinically active active compounds.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve (the early 20th-century botanical writer), A Modern Herbal, milk thistle entry: the hepatic tradition, the seed preparation. the herbal reference on Silybi mariani fructus, the evidence for liver support and the seed preparation. the World Health Organisation Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, Vol. 2, Fructus Silybi Mariae.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Crush seeds just before use; crushing releases the silymarin and starts the extraction. Dried whole milk thistle seeds store 2 years in a sealed tin.

glossaryTerms.definition: The complex of flavonolignans (silybin being the principal one) extracted from milk thistle seeds. The primary studied constituents associated with milk thistle's hepatoprotective and antioxidant actions in clinical research.
A herb that supports liver function. In western herbal medicine, milk thistle is the best-evidenced hepatic herb in the pharmacopoeia.

content.text: Milk thistle (Silybum marianum) is the best-studied
herb in the western herbal tradition. Its seeds contain

, a compound complex with strong evidence for liver support. It has been studied for chronic liver disease and liver injury from medication. The traditional use covers minor liver complaints and digestive symptoms. The preparation requires freshly crushed seeds and a 20-minute simmer.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Asteraceae allergy. Milk thistle is in the daisy family. Readers with a known ragweed, chrysanthemum, or daisy allergy should approach with caution.

Prescription medication. Milk thistle can affect the metabolism of drugs that depend on the CYP2C9 and P-glycoprotein pathways. Readers on hormone therapy, anticoagulants, or chemotherapy should consult their prescriber before regular use.

Liver disease. This preparation is for minor liver support after illness or medication use in an otherwise well reader. Diagnosed chronic liver disease, cirrhosis, hepatitis, jaundice, or any liver condition under medical management warrants specialist supervision for any herbal use.

items.prepNote: dried whole seeds; crush in a pestle and mortar just before use

content.text: Crush the dried milk thistle seeds in a pestle and mortar until roughly cracked open (not to powder; cracking is enough). Combine with cold water in a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer and maintain for 20 minutes. The liquid will turn slightly cloudy with a pale amber colour and a mild, slightly oily smell from the seed. Strain through a sieve, pressing the spent seed. Drink warm.
One cup (containing 5 g crushed seeds) once or twice a day. Take for 4-8 weeks; if symptoms persist, see your GP. Paediatric use: not for children under twelve without specialist advice.
Prepare each cup fresh. Crush seeds immediately before use. Whole dried seeds store 2 years in a sealed tin, cool and dark; once crushed, the seed oxidises and should be used within a few days.
When not to use this remedy

On hormone therapy, blood thinners, or cancer treatment without prescriber approval. Liver disease under medical management. Jaundice, right-side belly pain, or dark urine with pale stools needs urgent medical assessment.
